questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Young people who imagine that the life of the writer is one of joy or fame soon discover not only the difficulties of the calling but also the endless hostility and pressure of an unresponsive world. Upon being asked, "Aren't most editors failed writers?", T.S. Eliot replied, "Yes, but so are most writers." The statement by T.S. Eliot conveys which of the following ideas?

choices.a: That professions of editing can be just as creative and challenging as writing
choices.b: Writers are fortunate enough to attain real success in their profession
choices.c: For a writer, success is measured more by influence exerted in the material gain achieved
choices.d: Many writers find that a stint at editorial work is a beneficial apprenticeship in their craft
choices.e: There are no clear-cut standards of success and failure for writers, as there are such standards for editors

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Between 1960 and 1970, ivory poachers in the African nation of Zimbabwe killed over 10,000 elephants. During that period, the total elephant population in Zimbabwe fell from about 35,000 to just under 30,000. In 1970, severe poaching laws were implemented in Zimbabwe and between 1970 and 1980, over 800 poachers were arrested and expelled from the countryside. By 1980, the elephant population in Zimbabwe had fallen to about 21,000. Which of the following, if true, would best help to explain the apparent paradox presented above?

choices.a: The poachers arrested in Zimbabwe between 1970 and 1980 were not sentenced to long prison terms
choices.b: After 1980, there were well-publicized campaigns against the slaughter of elephants, demand for ivory fell between 1970 and 1980
choices.c: The elephant population in neighbouring Mombasa rose slightly between 1970 and 1980
choices.d: Before 1970, the anti-poaching laws passed by the parliament in Zimbabwe were rarely enforced
choices.e: In Zimbabwe, between 1970 and 1980, thousands of acres of forest, the elephant's natural habitat, were cleared for farming

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the going gets strong, flies are flown. If the sky is not clear, flies are flown. If the temperature is high, kites are flown. Assume the statements above to be true. If kites are flown, which of the following statements must be true?
